candidates.name: Cold Email Reply Analyzer
candidates.pitch: Provides unique tracking links and subjects per email variant, reports reply rates aggregated in a panel. Simple link cloaking + reply detection. Sales tooling market is growing rapidly.
candidates.pain: I send outreach sequences but have no visibility into which subject lines or copy actually get replies

candidates.name: AI Conversation Token Counter
candidates.pitch: Intercepts API calls to LLMs, tracks token counts per conversation and cumulatively. Displays running total in extension badge. Demand is exploding as AI usage becomes mainstream.
candidates.pain: I lose track of how many tokens I've used in AI conversations and can't budget for the month

candidates.name: JSON-LD Structured Data Inspector
candidates.pitch: Parses all JSON-LD from the page, validates against schema.org, and reports errors inline with the source. Simple DOM parsing + schema.org API lookup. Growing importance as SEO increasingly depends on
candidates.pain: Validating JSON-LD structured data requires external tools or manual copy-pasting to schema validators

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Kubernetes Pod Status Checker
candidates.pitch: Connects to k8s API with local config, surfaces pod status in extension badge. Requires some config but very actionable for devops. K8s adoption continues to grow.
candidates.pain: Checking Kubernetes pod status requires either kubectl access or logging into a cloud console

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Quota Usage Pulse
candidates.pitch: Parses API responses for rate limit headers, tracks usage over time, warns before exhaustion. Generic approach works across many APIs. Developer frustration with quotas is universal.
candidates.pain: API rate limits hit unexpectedly because I have no visibility into my usage against limits

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Email Tracking Pixel Detector
candidates.pitch: Scans rendered email content for 1x1 images and network requests matching known tracker domains. Browser-level analysis is novel. Privacy awareness is rising sharply post-GDPR.
candidates.pain: Marketing emails contain invisible tracking pixels that report when and where I opened them

candidates.name: Conference Lead Qualifier
candidates.pitch: Scans uploaded business cards or typed notes, enriches with LinkedIn/company data via public APIs. Mobile-first workflow with desktop enrichment. Trade show ROI tools are underserved.
candidates.pain: Collecting business cards at conferences leaves me with a pile of contacts and no context for outreach

candidates.name: LinkedIn Headline Optimizer
candidates.pitch: Analyzes current headline against engagement patterns from public data, suggests rewrites. Uses LLM for suggestions with A/B variant generation. Recruitment tools market is expanding.
candidates.pain: Writing LinkedIn headlines that get profile views requires guesswork and constant testing

candidates.name: AI Output History Vault
candidates.pitch: Captures and stores AI outputs to local IndexedDB with search and tagging. Simple overlay panel for recall. Growing need as people use AI for substantive work.
candidates.pain: My AI chat history gets lost when tabs close and there's no persistent scratchpad for important outputs

candidates.name: AI Prompt Diff Viewer
candidates.pitch: Creates lightweight diffs between consecutive prompts sent to AI APIs, stores locally. Side-by-side diff view in panel. Directly useful for power users tweaking prompts.
candidates.pain: Iterating on AI prompts without version history means losing track of what worked and what didn't

candidates.name: Page Scroll Heatmap Generator
candidates.pitch: Tracks scroll depth buckets across sessions, generates visual heatmap overlay. Local-only storage, exportable reports. Simpler alternative to full analytics suites for small sites.
candidates.pain: Understanding where visitors scroll on my landing page requires expensive analytics tools

candidates.name: API Latency Scatterplot
candidates.pitch: Captures network request timing and plots against query parameter combinations to spot latency patterns. Uses built-in Performance API and a simple D3 scatterplot. Niche but high value for backend-hea
candidates.pain: Slow API endpoints are hard to correlate with request parameters without custom logging infrastructure

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Dead Code Path Detector
candidates.pitch: Instruments the page to log which conditional branches execute, then surfaces unused paths in a DevTools panel. Buildable as a content script with runtime instrumentation. Emerging interest in code qu
candidates.pain: Production JavaScript bundles contain dead code paths that are impossible to trace without runtime analysis

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Third-Party Script Cost Reporter
candidates.pitch: Uses Resource Timing API to report each external script's download time, execution time, and blocking impact. Lightweight panel overlay. Supply chain security concerns are driving demand for script au
candidates.pain: Third-party scripts silently bloat page weight and block rendering but impact is invisible until production

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: DOM Mutation Reporter
candidates.pitch: Hooks MutationObserver to log all DOM mutations with stack traces and timestamps. Helps debug React/Vue/Svelte re-render cascades. Directly useful for performance debugging.
candidates.pain: JavaScript frameworks dynamically update the DOM and there's no easy way to track what changed and why

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: External Link Auditor
candidates.pitch: Validates all external links on a page on demand, reports status codes in a sortable table. Fetch API + batch checking. QA tooling demand remains steady.
candidates.pain: Checking if external links are broken requires clicking each one or running separate crawler tools

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link
